experiences.intentions: The left box at (2,1) is directly below the target at (1,1), and pushing it up would solve one target immediately without deadlock because the destination is a target. The player is at (2,2), adjacent to that box on its right, so a push left would only jam it into the wall corner at (2,0) which is impossible anyway, while moving around wastes turns and risks oscillation. Best action is to push the box upward onto the target by first getting below it? Wait: from (2,2) I am to the right of the box, so the only immediate push is left, which would be bad. I should instead reposition to get below the box. From current layout, moving down to (3,2) is blocked by wall at (3,2). Moving up to (1,2) is open and is the first step toward getting left/up corridor access to eventually push the other box or approach the left box from below via row 5 path. Since oscillation happened recently, choose a purposeful repositioning move: up.
